name: gates-config-driven-external-tools
tags: gates external tools yaml config driven separation mcp local-mac prereq task:d4b214d0
body:
External MCP tool gates (local-mac tools like imessage__send, mail__compose, mail__delete) are declared in ~/.claude/gate_rules.yaml — not as Python classes in gates.py. The loader _load_external_gates() reads the YAML at module import and merges into GATES. Internal gates (git, tasks — need DB access) stay as code.

**Why:** McpHookBridgeNode pattern — engine in claude-hooks, rules owned by the tool's repo/config. No Python change needed to gate a new external tool.

**How to apply:** To gate a new local-mac tool, add an entry to ~/.claude/gate_rules.yaml with tool, prereq, window_s, and optionally name_arg or input_arg. Never add a concrete Gate subclass for an external tool.
